sentence: At some point -- as I've said in the last two press conferences -- it will become appropriate to slow the pace of increases as we approach the level of interest rates that will be sufficiently restrictive to bring inflation down to our 2 percent goal.

Let me emphasize again that today's modification of the forward guidance should not be read as indicating that the Committee has decided on the timing of the initial increase in the target range for the federal funds rate.
While the jobs-to-workers gap has narrowed, labor demand still substantially exceeds the supply of available workers.
As you know, in normal times, the Committee eases monetary policy by lowering the target for the short-term policy interest rate, the federal funds rate.
We know that reducing policy restraint too soon or too much could result in a reversal of the progress that we've seen on inflation.
